DIEPENBROCK, WULF, PLANT & HANNEGAN
LAW OFFICES

300 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 1700
POST OFFICE BOX 3034
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95812-3034

(916) 444-3910

(916) 446-1696

FORREST A. PLANT
JOHN V. DIEPENBROCK

CATHERINE M. ALLEN
SUE ELLEN WOOLDRIDGE
TRACEY S. BUCK-WALSH
ANNETTE MCKINNEY-DAVIS
KATHLEEN B. MAKEI
BRADLEY J. ELKIN
JOHN M. FELDER
PATRICIA SHARP-TAYLOR
RICHARD G. ROSENFELD
THOMAS A. WILLOUGHBY

MAUREEN COLEMAN MOORE
BRUCE C. CLINE
DAVID E. MERRILL
PAUL W. TAPPER
HELD. OPPHAUER CORDEIRO
TIMOTHY S. ABIETZ
MARK D. HARRISON
MARY J. MARTINELLI
GARY A. BERGER
WILLIAM W. PALMER
MICHAEL R. KNUDSEN
JAMES W. CLARK
SEAN O. SHERIDAN
PAUL J. PASCUZZI
MARK O'CONNOR
RICHARD K. VOSS
MICHAEL DROIT
D. JEFFERY GRIMES

February 5, 1991


Scott Hallibran, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807


Dear Mr. Hallibran:

     This letter is to request written advice pursuant to
Government Code section 83114 on behalf of the Lungren for
Attorney General 1994 committee.

                    STATEMENT OF FACTS

     Dan Lungren was elected Attorney General at the November 1990
general election.  His controlled committee, Lungren for Attorney
General 1990, concluded the year ending December 31, 1990 with
cash on hand exceeding its accrued expenses.  After the November
1990 election, but before the Attorney General-elect was sworn in,
his campaign staff, specifically Hayden Dawson, spoke with, to the
best of Mr. Dawson's recollection, attorney John Wallace at the
FPPC about the technical requirements needed to form the Lungren
for Attorney General 1994 committee.  Mr. Wallace told Mr. Dawson

DIEPENBROCK, WULFF, PLANT & HANNEGAN

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
February 5, 1991
Page - 2 -

that the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee could use the
same bank account and ID number as the Lungren for Attorney
General 1990 committee.

Separately, an employee of the Lungren for Attorney General
1990 committee sought advice from an attorney who advised that
staff member that, based on that attorney's conversations with the
FPPC, the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee should open
a new bank account but could use the same identification number.

Based on these two sets of advice, the Lungren for Attorney
General 1994 committee was formed, forms 501 and 502 were
completed and filed and a new bank account was opened.  The
Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee has ordered printed
material with the "old" ID number imprinted.

On February 4, 1991, Susan Bobrow, an attorney with the FPPC,
advised me that if the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee
had opened a new bank account, a separate ID number should be
obtained from the Secretary of State.

QUESTION

The Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee wishes to
comply with the provisions of the Political Reform Act as amended
and therefore formally requests an advice letter on whether it
needs to obtain a new ID number for this new committee.

DIEPENBROCK, WULFF, PLANT & HANNEGAN

Scott Hallibran, Esq.
February 5, 1991
Page - 3 -


Please call if you have any questions.

Very truly yours,

DIEPENBROCK, WULFF, PLANT
& HANNEGAN

By [signature]
TRACEY S. BUCK-WALSH

His controlled committee, Lungren for Attorney General 1990, concluded the year ending December 31, 1990 with cash on hand exceeding its accrued expenses. that the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee could use the same bank account and ID number as the Lungren for Attorney General 1990 committee.

Separately, an employee of the Lungren for Attorney General 1990 committee sought advice from an attorney who advised that staff member that, based on that attorney's conversations with the FPPC, the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee should open a new bank account but could use the same identification number.

Based on these two sets of advice, the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee was formed, forms 501 and 502 were completed and filed and a new bank account was opened. The Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee has ordered printed material with the "old" ID number imprinted.

On February 4, 1991, Susan Bobrow, an attorney with the FPPC, advised me that if the Lungren for Attorney General 1994 committee had opened a new bank account, a separate ID number should be obtained from the Secretary of State.
